title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases · C Contract Brief: Dangling Interior Pointer
summary: An asynchronous job queue keeps aliases after cancellation and occasionally corrupts a newly allocated job at the same address. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem dangling interior pointer increment in generation-checked handle repair. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
